title: Notice: Glucagon for injection supply in Canada - Canada.ca
content:
## Glucagon for injection supply in Canada: Notice
Date published: May 29, 2026
Glucagon for injection is used for the emergency treatment of severe hypoglycemia (low blood sugar).
Glucagon for injection was previously marketed in Canada by Novo Nordisk Canada Inc. under the brand name GlucaGen/GlucaGen HypoKit, and Eli Lilly Canada Inc. under the brand name Glucagon. Novo Nordisk and Eli Lilly discontinued the sale of these drugs, and all inventory has been fully depleted.
We recognize the importance of glucagon for injection for patients and we have taken steps to support its availability in Canada.
Beginning in May 2023, we allowed Amphastar Pharmaceuticals Inc. to import US-authorized Glucagon for injection. The company is permitted to do so until July 1, 2026. All imported foreign-authorized drugs can be sold until their expiry date and we are closely monitoring supply. For more information, visit the List of drugs for exceptional importation and sale .
Allowing the importation of foreign-authorized drugs is meant as a short-term measure to help mitigate the impacts of a shortage.
Regarding long-term access to a Canadian-labelled drug, a submission for glucagon for injection is currently under review with Health Canada. For more information, visit Drug and Health Product Submissions Under Review (SUR): New drug submissions under review - Canada.ca .
We are communicating with patient and health care professional associations and provincial and territorial governments to share information about the supply of glucagon for injection.
Check this webpage regularly as we will update it if new information becomes available.
Learn more about drug shortages in Canada and how Canadians can deal with drug shortages .
